From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Trade union

Amalgamated Textile Workers' Union

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Merged into | [General, Municipal, Boilermakers and Allied Trades Union](/wiki/General,_Municipal,_Boilermakers_and_Allied_Trades_Union "General, Municipal, Boilermakers and Allied Trades Union") |
| Founded | 1 January 1974 |
| Dissolved | 1985 |
| Headquarters | Textile Union Centre, 5 Caton Street, [Rochdale](/wiki/Rochdale "Rochdale") |
| Location | * [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") |
| Members | 19,500 (1983) |
| Affiliations | [TUC](/wiki/Trades_Union_Congress "Trades Union Congress"), [ITGLWF](/wiki/International_Textile,_Garment_and_Leather_Workers%27_Federation "International Textile, Garment and Leather Workers' Federation") |

The **Amalgamated Textile Workers' Union** (**ATWU**) was a [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") in [Great Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain "Great Britain").[[1]](#cite_note-henderson-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Amalgamated_Textile_Workers%27_Union&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The union was founded in 1974, when the [Amalgamated Weavers' Association](/wiki/Amalgamated_Weavers%27_Association "Amalgamated Weavers' Association") merged with the [National Union of Textile and Allied Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_Textile_and_Allied_Workers "National Union of Textile and Allied Workers").[[2]](#cite_note-2) The [Amalgamated Textile Warehousemen's Association](/wiki/Amalgamated_Textile_Warehousemen%27s_Association "Amalgamated Textile Warehousemen's Association") developed close links with the new union, and the two shared a general secretary.[[3]](#cite_note-directory-3)

In 1983, the important [Burnley, Nelson, Rossendale and District Textile Workers' Union](/wiki/Burnley,_Nelson,_Rossendale_and_District_Textile_Workers%27_Union "Burnley, Nelson, Rossendale and District Textile Workers' Union") decided to leave the ATWU, and argued that as its largest affiliate, it should be entitled to a proportionate share of the union's funds. The ATWU disagreed, and the dispute went to the [High Court of England and Wales](/wiki/High_Court_of_England_and_Wales "High Court of England and Wales"), which rejected the Burnley and Nelson union's claim.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

With widespread redundancies in the industry, the union lost two-thirds of its members before it merged into the [General, Municipal, Boilermakers and Allied Trades Union](/wiki/General,_Municipal,_Boilermakers_and_Allied_Trades_Union "General, Municipal, Boilermakers and Allied Trades Union") in 1985.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Based in [Rochdale](/wiki/Rochdale "Rochdale"), the union's final general secretary was Jack Brown.[[1]](#cite_note-henderson-1)

## Affiliates

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Amalgamated_Textile_Workers%27_Union&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Affiliates")]

By 1982, the union had the following affiliates:[[6]](#cite_note-6)

:   [Blackburn and District Weavers', Winders' and Warpers' Association](/wiki/Blackburn_and_District_Weavers%27,_Winders%27_and_Warpers%27_Association "Blackburn and District Weavers', Winders' and Warpers' Association")
:   [Bolton and District Union of Textile and Allied Workers](/wiki/Bolton_and_District_Union_of_Textile_and_Allied_Workers "Bolton and District Union of Textile and Allied Workers")
:   [Bolton and District Weavers', Winders' and Warpers' Association](/wiki/Bolton_and_District_Weavers%27,_Winders%27_and_Warpers%27_Association "Bolton and District Weavers', Winders' and Warpers' Association")
:   [Burnley, Nelson, Rossendale and District Textile Workers' Union](/wiki/Burnley,_Nelson,_Rossendale_and_District_Textile_Workers%27_Union "Burnley, Nelson, Rossendale and District Textile Workers' Union")
:   Colne and Craven Textile Workers' Association
:   [Oldham Provincial Union of Textile and Allied Workers](/w/index.php?title=Oldham_Provincial_Union_of_Textile_and_Allied_Workers&action=edit&redlink=1 "Oldham Provincial Union of Textile and Allied Workers (page does not exist)")
:   [National Union of Textile and Allied Workers, Rochdale and Districts](/w/index.php?title=National_Union_of_Textile_and_Allied_Workers,_Rochdale_and_Districts&action=edit&redlink=1 "National Union of Textile and Allied Workers, Rochdale and Districts (page does not exist)")
:   [Northern Textile and Allied Workers' Union](/wiki/Northern_Textile_and_Allied_Workers%27_Union "Northern Textile and Allied Workers' Union")
:   [North Lancashire and Cumbria Textile Workers' Association](/wiki/North_Lancashire_and_Cumbria_Textile_Workers%27_Association "North Lancashire and Cumbria Textile Workers' Association")
:   [North West Lancashire, Durham and Cumbria Textile Workers' Union](/wiki/North_West_Lancashire,_Durham_and_Cumbria_Textile_Workers%27_Union "North West Lancashire, Durham and Cumbria Textile Workers' Union")
:   Rochdale and Todmorden District of the Amalgamated Textile Workers' Union
:   Southern Area
:   Staff Section
:   Wigan

## Leadership

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Amalgamated_Textile_Workers%27_Union&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Leadership")]

### General Secretaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Amalgamated_Textile_Workers%27_Union&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: General Secretaries")]

:   1974: [Fred Hague](/wiki/Fred_Hague "Fred Hague") and [Joe King](/wiki/Joe_King_(trade_unionist) "Joe King (trade unionist)")
:   1975: Fred Hague
:   1976: [Jack Brown](/wiki/Jack_Brown_(trade_unionist) "Jack Brown (trade unionist)")

### Presidents

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Amalgamated_Textile_Workers%27_Union&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Presidents")]

:   1974: [Jim Browning](/wiki/Jim_Browning_(trade_unionist) "Jim Browning (trade unionist)")
:   1976: Joe Quinn
:   1984: Albert Hilton
